body: Issue date: May 19, 2023 The lack of visibility into some licensed or authorized cannabis stores is putting cannabis store operators, staff and patrons at risk. The Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) is repealing the visibility requirements from the Cannabis Licensing Regulation (CLR). Removing the requirement in s. 5 (1)(p.1) of the CLR will improve the visibility into cannabis stores and increase safety for store operators, staff and patrons. Display rules in the federal Cannabis Act are still in place. The LCRB will instead have a term and condition prohibiting window displays of cannabis and cannabis accessories to people outside the store. This change will also provide licensee and authorization holders more flexibility, while ensuring that cannabis and cannabis accessories will not be displayed to people outside the store, in keeping with the federal Cannabis Act. Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws. When interpreting and applying the information contained in the Bulletins, you are encouraged to seek advice from your professional advisors as appropriate in the circumstances. The B.C. Public Service acknowledges the territories of First Nations around B.C. and is grateful to carry out our work on these lands. We acknowledge the rights, interests, priorities, and concerns of all Indigenous Peoples - First Nations, Métis, and Inuit - respecting and acknowledging their distinct cultures, histories, rights, laws, and governments.

minutes_briefs.title: BC repeals cannabis store visibility rules to improve safety
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) has removed the requirement that cannabis stores must have limited visibility from outside. This change is meant to make stores safer for operators, staff, and customers by allowing more natural light and visibility into the store. However, stores still cannot display cannabis or cannabis accessories in windows where people outside can see them. The federal Cannabis Act display rules still apply.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check your store's windows to ensure no cannabis or accessories are visible from outside, as required by the new term and condition.
